href=\"https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eClaude Design\u003c/a\u003e alternative.\u003c/strong\u003e  🖥️ \u003cstrong\u003eNative desktop app for macOS and Windows.\u003c/strong\u003e  ⚡ \u003cstrong\u003e100+ skills\u003c/strong\u003e · ✨ \u003cstrong\u003e150 brand-grade \u003ccode\u003eDESIGN.md\u003c/code\u003e systems\u003c/strong\u003e · 📦 \u003cstrong\u003e261 ready-to-use plugins.\u003c/strong\u003e  🖼️ Generates \u003cstrong\u003eweb · desktop · mobile prototypes\u003c/strong\u003e, \u003cstrong\u003elive dashboards / artifacts\u003c/strong\u003e, \u003cstrong\u003edecks\u003c/strong\u003e, \u003cstrong\u003eimages\u003c/strong\u003e, \u003cstrong\u003evideo\u003c/strong\u003e, plus \u003cstrong\u003eHyperFrames\u003c/strong\u003e motion graphics. 🔒 Sandboxed iframe preview · HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 export.  🤖 \u003cstrong\u003eRuns on Claude Code · OpenClaw · Codex · Cursor · OpenCode · Qwen · Copilot · Amp · Hermes · Kimi · Antigravity and 22 local CLIs\u003c/strong\u003e, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via BYOK.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpen Design is what you get when the \u003cstrong\u003eagent-native\u003c/strong\u003e loop Anthropic shipped with Claude Design — discover the brief, lock the direction, stream the artifact, critique, deliver — stops being closed and becomes a \u003cstrong\u003efilesystem of skills, design systems, and plugins\u003c/strong\u003e that the coding agents already on your laptop can read, write, and remix. Your CLI becomes the design engine, your laptop becomes the studio, and your team's \u003ccode\u003eDESIGN.md\u003c/code\u003e becomes the brand contract.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIt's also the \u003cstrong\u003eFigma alternative for the agent era\u003c/strong\u003e — instead of pushing pixels on a canvas, it delivers single-page artifacts in real CSS, real fonts, real components, exported straight to HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 — already shaped by your design system, already runnable inside the agent you use every day.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eProduct tour\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-product-tour\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Product tour\" href=\"#product-tour\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA quick look at what Open Design is and what it does. /api/proxy/{anthropic,openai,azure,google,ollama,senseaudio}/stream\u003c/code\u003e gives you the same loop (no process spawn) — paste \u003ccode\u003ebaseUrl\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003eapiKey\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003emodel\u003c/code\u003e, with support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. The OD-specific render workflow (composition cache, sandbox-exec workaround, MP4-as-chip) is detailed in \u003ca href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/design-templates/hyperframes\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003edesign-templates/hyperframes/\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhy Open Design\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-why-open-design\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Why Open Design\" href=\"#why-open-design\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIn April 2026, Anthropic released \u003ca href=\"https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eClaude Design\u003c/a\u003e — the first time an LLM stopped writing prose and started delivering design artifacts directly.\u003c/strong\u003e It went viral. But it stayed closed-source, paid-only, cloud-only, locked to Anthropic's model, Anthropic's skills, Anthropic's surface. No checkout, no self-host, no Vercel deploy, no swap-in-your-own-agent.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpen Design (OD) is the open-source alternative. Same loop, same artifact-first mental model, none of the lock-in:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🤖 \u003cstrong\u003eAgent-native, model-agnostic.\u003c/strong\u003e We don't ship an agent. The \u003ccode\u003eclaude\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ecodex\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ecursor-agent\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ecopilot\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ehermes\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ekimi\u003c/code\u003e already on your \u003ccode\u003ePATH\u003c/code\u003e are the design engine. Swap with one click.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🧠 \u003cstrong\u003eBrand-grade by default.\u003c/strong\u003e Every render reads the active \u003ccode\u003eDESIGN.md\u003c/code\u003e — a 9-section schema covering palette, type, spacing, motion, voice, anti-patterns. 150 systems ship with the repo (Linear, Stripe, Vercel, Airbnb, Apple, Tesla, Notion, Anthropic, Cursor, Supabase, Figma…). Drop a folder in, the picker finds it.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🖥️ \u003cstrong\u003eLocal-first, BYOK at every layer.\u003c/strong\u003e Native desktop apps for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) and Windows (x64). Linux AppImage on the optional release lane. SQLite at \u003ccode\u003e.od/app.sqlite\u003c/code\u003e, files at \u003ccode\u003e.od/projects/\u0026lt;id\u0026gt;/\u003c/code\u003e, no telemetry, no cloud round-trip.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🌍 \u003cstrong\u003eComposable on three planes.\u003c/strong\u003e \u003cstrong\u003ePlugins\u003c/strong\u003e carry runnable workflows · \u003cstrong\u003eskills\u003c/strong\u003e carry the agent's design taste · \u003cstrong\u003edesign systems\u003c/strong\u003e carry the brand. All three are plain files anyone can author, version, and publish.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🔁 \u003cstrong\u003eRefresh an existing codebase.\u003c/strong\u003e Hand a \u003ccode\u003egit\u003c/code\u003e repo + \u003ccode\u003eDESIGN.md\u003c/code\u003e to the agent and it refactors your real components to the brand spec. Dedicated plugins migrate Figma / Pencil workflows into React / Next.js / Vue code.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🔒 \u003cstrong\u003ePrivacy by conviction.\u003c/strong\u003e Everything runs where your data lives — your laptop, your team's server, your Vercel project. When the network is needed, the BYOK proxy is SSRF-guarded.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" Full quickstart, env vars, Nix flake, and packaged build flow → \u003ca href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/QUICKSTART.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eQUICKSTART.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eA full workflow — from brief to artifact\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-a-full-workflow--from-brief-to-artifact\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: A full workflow — from brief to artifact\" href=\"#a-full-workflow--from-brief-to-artifact\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebrief → plugin → direction → design system → artifact → handoff → memory\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA PM submits a brief.\u003c/strong\u003e The plugin picker offers landing page · pitch deck · dashboard · social post · PM spec · OKR scorecard…\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eA designer (or the agent) locks the direction.\u003c/strong\u003e No brand? Pick from 5 curated directions. Have a brand? Drop a screenshot / URL → the agent connects GitHub, imports Figma, and codifies a reusable \u003ccode\u003eDESIGN.md\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eThe agent emits the first \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;artifact\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/strong\u003e Plugin + skill + \u003ccode\u003eDESIGN.md\u003c/code\u003e are bound. It streams into a sandboxed iframe, editable in place — not \"regenerate from scratch.\"\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHand off to engineering.\u003c/strong\u003e The artifact is real HTML/CSS — drop it into Cursor, Codex, or Claude Code to keep building as code. Or export PPTX / PDF / MP4 straight to marketing.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOpen Design gets smarter as you use it.\u003c/strong\u003e Your screenshots, fonts, palettes, and confirmed artifacts accumulate as defaults for the next session. Less rework, less drift.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eUse Open Design from your coding agent\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-use-open-design-from-your-coding-agent\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Use Open Design from your coding agent\" href=\"#use-open-design-from-your-coding-agent\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpen Design ships a \u003cstrong\u003estdio MCP server\u003c/strong\u003e and per-agent \u003cstrong\u003einstall scripts\u003c/strong\u003e. Any MCP-compatible agent in another repo can read files from your local Open Design projects directly — tokens CSS, JSX components, entry HTML — as a structured API queryable by name. The agent always sees the live file, not a stale export.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"# One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):\ncurl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh | sh -s \u0026lt;agent\u0026gt;\n\n# Then the agent can:\nod search-files \u0026quot;primary button\u0026quot;      # search files across projects\nod get-file design-systems/linear-app/DESIGN.md\nod get-artifact \u0026lt;slug\u0026gt;                # latest rendered artifact\nod plugin run web-prototype --brief \u0026quot;...\u0026quot;\nod skill list --scenario marketing\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):\u003c/span\u003e\ncurl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e|\u003c/span\u003e sh -s \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026lt;\u003c/span\u003eagent\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026gt;\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Then the agent can:\u003c/span\u003e\nod search-files \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003eprimary button\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e search files across projects\u003c/span\u003e\nod get-file design-systems/linear-app/DESIGN.md\nod get-artifact \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026lt;\u003c/span\u003eslug\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026gt;\u003c/span\u003e                \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e latest rendered artifact\u003c/span\u003e\nod plugin run web-prototype --brief \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e...\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\nod skill list --scenario marketing\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhy MCP?\u003c/strong\u003e Exporting and re-attaching a zip every iteration breaks flow. MCP exposes the design source directly — the agent always sees the live file.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFor an agent starting from scratch,\u003c/strong\u003e the installer places \u003ccode\u003e~/.config/\u0026lt;agent\u0026gt;/open-design.json\u003c/code\u003e (or the platform equivalent) plus a copy-paste MCP snippet. Cursor gets a one-click deeplink; Claude Code gets a \u003ccode\u003eclaude mcp add-json\u003c/code\u003e one-liner; every other agent gets JSON in the schema its config expects. Full per-agent flow → \u003cstrong\u003eSettings → MCP server\u003c/strong\u003e in the desktop app, or \u003ca href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/agent-adapters.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003edocs/agent-adapters.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSecurity model.\u003c/strong\u003e Read-only by default, the daemon binds to \u003ccode\u003e127.0.0.1\u003c/code\u003e, and SSRF is blocked at the proxy edge. LAN exposure requires an explicit \u003ccode\u003eOD_BIND_HOST\u003c/code\u003e plus \u003ccode\u003eOD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS\u003c/code\u003e. Connector credentials and live-artifact preview routes stay loopback-only regardless.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSkills\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-skills\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Skills\" href=\"#skills\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e100+ skills ship in the box\u003c/strong\u003e — each is a folder under \u003ca href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/skills\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eskills/\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e following the Claude Code \u003ca href=\"https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/skills\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eSKILL.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e convention, extended with an \u003ccode\u003eod:\u003c/code\u003e frontmatter (\u003ccode\u003emode\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eplatform\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003escenario\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003epreview.type\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003edesign_system.requires\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003edefault_for\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003efidelity\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eexample_prompt\u003c/code\u003e). Drop a folder in, restart the daemon, it appears in the picker.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTwo \u003cstrong\u003emodes\u003c/strong\u003e anchor the catalog: \u003ccode\u003eprototype\u003c/code\u003e (web/mobile/desktop single-page artifacts) and \u003ccode\u003edeck\u003c/code\u003e (horizontal-swipe presentations). Also \u003ccode\u003eimage\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003evideo\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eaudio\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003etemplate\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003edesign-system\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode\u003eutility\u003c/code\u003e modes. The \u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003escenario\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e field groups them by audience: \u003ccode\u003edesign\u003c/code\u003e · \u003ccode\u003emarketing\u003c/code\u003e · \u003ccode\u003eoperation\u003c/code\u003e · \u003ccode\u003eengineering\u003c/code\u003e · \u003ccode\u003eproduct\u003c/code\u003e · \u003ccode\u003efinance\u003c/code\u003e · minimum\u003c/strong\u003e; to list it in the Open Design marketplace, add an \u003ccode\u003eopen-design.json\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"my-plugin/\n├── SKILL.md            ← required: YAML frontmatter (name · description) + trigger phrasing + workflow (aim for \u0026lt; 500 lines)\n├── open-design.json    ← needed to list: marketplace metadata + inputs + pipeline + capabilities\n├── README.md           ← optional: usage, install, registry links\n├── preview/            ← optional: index.html / poster.png (strongly recommended for visual plugins)\n└── examples/           ← optional: concrete use cases\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003emy-plugin/\n├── SKILL.md            ← required: YAML frontmatter (name · description) + trigger phrasing + workflow (aim for \u0026lt; 500 lines)\n├── open-design.json    ← needed to list: marketplace metadata + inputs + pipeline + capabilities\n├── README.md           ← optional: usage, install, registry links\n├── preview/            ← optional: index.html / poster.png (strongly recommended for visual plugins)\n└── examples/           ← optional: concrete use cases\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCore \u003ccode\u003eopen-design.json\u003c/code\u003e fields: \u003ccode\u003especVersion\u003c/code\u003e (currently \u003ccode\u003e1.0.0\u003c/code\u003e), \u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e (stable ID), \u003ccode\u003eversion\u003c/code\u003e (semver), \u003ccode\u003ecompat.agentSkills[].path\u003c/code\u003e (points at \u003ccode\u003e./SKILL.md\u003c/code\u003e), \u003ccode\u003eod.kind\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eskill\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003escenario\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eatom\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ebundle\u003c/code\u003e), \u003ccode\u003eod.taskKind\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003enew-generation\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003efigma-migration\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ecode-migration\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003etune-collab\u003c/code\u003e), \u003ccode\u003eod.mode\u003c/code\u003e (the output surface, e.g. \u003ccode\u003eprototype\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003edeck\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003elive-artifact\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eimage\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003evideo\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ehyperframes\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eaudio\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003edesign-system\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003escenario\u003c/code\u003e), \u003ccode\u003eod.capabilities[]\u003c/code\u003e (\u003cstrong\u003edeclare the minimum\u003c/strong\u003e — a restricted install grants only \u003ccode\u003eprompt:inject\u003c/code\u003e by default), \u003ccode\u003eod.inputs[]\u003c/code\u003e (apply-time parameters).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eScaffold + validate Find a good-first-issue and assign yourself\ngh issue list --label \u0026quot;good first issue\u0026quot; --state open --limit 20\ngh issue develop \u0026lt;number\u0026gt;   # create a branch and worktree\n\n# 3. Make the change, run the checks\npnpm guard \u0026amp;\u0026amp; pnpm typecheck\npnpm --filter @open-design/\u0026lt;package\u0026gt; test\n\n# 4. Find a good-first-issue and assign yourself\u003c/span\u003e\ngh issue list --label \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003egood first issue\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e --state open --limit 20\ngh issue develop \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026lt;\u003c/span\u003enumber\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026gt;\u003c/span\u003e   \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e create a branch and worktree\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e 3. lives in one window — go from a vague idea to discovering references, gathering material, editing interactively, queuing comments, polishing motion, and handing off to an editor or a Code Agent — **Native desktop app for macOS and Windows.** \u0026nbsp;⚡ **100+ skills** · ✨ **150 brand-grade `DESIGN.md` systems** · 📦 **261 ready-to-use plugins.** \u0026nbsp;🖼️ Generates **web · desktop · mobile prototypes**, **live dashboards / artifacts**, **decks**, **images**, **video**, plus **HyperFrames** motion graphics. 🔒 Sandboxed iframe preview · HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 export. \u0026nbsp;🤖 **Runs on Claude Code · OpenClaw · Codex · Cursor · OpenCode · Qwen · Copilot · Amp · Hermes · Kimi · Antigravity and 22 local CLIs**, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via BYOK.","","Open Design is what you get when the **agent-native** loop Anthropic shipped with Claude Design — discover the brief, lock the direction, stream the artifact, critique, deliver — stops being closed and becomes a **filesystem of skills, design systems, and plugins** that the coding agents already on your laptop can read, write, and remix. Your CLI becomes the design engine, your laptop becomes the studio, and your team's `DESIGN.md` becomes the brand contract.","","It's also the **Figma alternative for the agent era** — instead of pushing pixels on a canvas, it delivers single-page artifacts in real CSS, real fonts, real components, exported straight to HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 — already shaped by your design system, already runnable inside the agent you use every day.","","[cd]: https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649","","---","","## Product tour","","A quick look at what Open Design is and what it does. Start from **Home**, orchestrate repeat workflows with **Automation**, distill a brand contract in **Design System**, and extend with **Plugins** and **integrations**; inside any project's **Studio**, the same design system streams out prototypes, live artifacts, HyperFrames, decks, and images.","","### Core pages","","\u003ctable\u003e","\u003ctr\u003e","\u003ctd valign=\"top\"\u003e","\u003cimg src=\"https://repo-assets.open-design.ai/resources/images/product/home.png\" alt=\"Home page\" /\u003e\u003cbr/\u003e","\u003csub\u003e\u003cb\u003eHome\u003c/b\u003e — the overview entry point. ships as **skills, a CLI, and an MCP server** that mainstream coding agents consume natively. Once OD is installed, a single `od mcp install \u003cagent\u003e` wires the MCP server into that agent's config, and you call the same tools from inside any agent.","","| Coding agent / platform \u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp; | Status \u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp; | One-line MCP server install \u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp;\u0026nbsp; |","|---|:---:|---|","| [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) | ✅ Supported | `od mcp install claude` |","| [Codex CLI](https://github.com/openai/codex) | ✅ Supported | `od mcp list with `od mcp install --help`.","","\u003cp align=\"center\"\u003e","  \u003cimg src=\"https://repo-assets.open-design.ai/resources/images/coding-agents.png\" alt=\"The 21 coding-agent CLIs Open Reasonix · Aider · Devin · Trae\" width=\"100%\" /\u003e","\u003c/p\u003e","","**No CLI installed?** The BYOK proxy at `POST /api/proxy/{anthropic,openai,azure,google,ollama,senseaudio}/stream` gives you the same loop (no process spawn) — paste `baseUrl` + `apiKey` + `model`, with support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Per-target SSRF protection blocks internal IPs / link-local / CGNAT at the daemon edge.","","The adapter contract and stream parsers live in [`apps/daemon/src/agents.ts`](apps/daemon/src/agents.ts). Adding a new CLI is one entry — see [`docs/agent-adapters.md`](docs/agent-adapters.md).","","---","","## Demo","","Four core product categories, all rendered by a coding agent running on your laptop. The OD-specific render workflow (composition cache, sandbox-exec workaround, MP4-as-chip) is detailed in [`design-templates/hyperframes/`](design-templates/hyperframes/).","","[hyperframes]: https://github.com/heygen-com/hyperframes","","---","","## Why Open Design","","\u003e **In April 2026, Anthropic released [Claude Design][cd] — the first time an LLM stopped writing prose and started delivering design artifacts directly.** It went viral. But it stayed closed-source, paid-only, cloud-only, locked to Anthropic's model, Anthropic's skills, Anthropic's surface. No checkout, no self-host, no Vercel deploy, no swap-in-your-own-agent.","","Open Design (OD) is the open-source alternative. Same loop, same artifact-first mental model, none of the lock-in:","","- 🤖 **Agent-native, model-agnostic.** We don't ship an agent. The `claude` / `codex` / `cursor-agent` / `copilot` / `hermes` / `kimi` already on your `PATH` are the design engine. Swap with one click.","- 🧠 **Brand-grade by default.** Every render reads the active `DESIGN.md` — a 9-section schema covering palette, type, spacing, motion, voice, anti-patterns. 150 systems ship with the repo (Linear, Stripe, Vercel, Airbnb, Apple, Tesla, Notion, Anthropic, Cursor, Supabase, Figma…). Drop a folder in, the picker finds it.","- 🖥️ **Local-first, BYOK at every layer.** Native desktop apps for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) and Windows (x64). Linux AppImage on the optional release lane. SQLite at `.od/app.sqlite`, files at `.od/projects/\u003cid\u003e/`, no telemetry, no cloud round-trip.","- 🌍 **Composable on three planes.** **Plugins** carry runnable workflows · **skills** carry the agent's design taste · **design systems** carry the brand. All three are plain files anyone can author, version, and publish.","- 🔁 **Refresh an existing codebase.** Hand a `git` repo + `DESIGN.md` to the agent and it refactors your real components to the brand spec. Dedicated plugins migrate Figma / Pencil workflows into React / Next.js / Vue code.","- 🔒 **Privacy by conviction.** Everything runs where your data lives — your laptop, your team's server, your Vercel project. When the network is needed, the BYOK proxy is SSRF-guarded.","","### Comparison","","| | [Claude Design][cd] | Figma | Lovable / v0 / Bolt | **Open Design** |","|---|---|---|---|---|","| Open source | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | **✅ Apache-2.0** |","| Self-host / desktop | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | **✅ macOS + Windows + Vercel** |","| Agent-native (runs in your CLI) | Anthropic only | ❌ | Cloud agent only | **✅ 22 CLIs + BYOK** |","| Brand-grade `DESIGN.md` | Proprietary | Theme JSON | Limited tokens | **✅ 150 systems shipped** |","| Skills / plugins / templates | Closed | Plugin store | Closed | **✅ 100+ skills · 261 plugins** |","| HyperFrames (HTML→MP4) | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | **✅ First-class** |","| Refresh an existing repo to brand | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | **✅ via agent + `DESIGN.md`** |","| Minimum billing | Pro / Max / Team | Pro / Org | Pro / Team | **BYOK · any compatible endpoint** |","","---","","## Quick start","","### 🖥️ Download the desktop app (recommended — zero config)","","The fastest way to use Open Design. No Node, no pnpm, no clone.","","- **macOS** (Apple Silicon · Intel x64) → [**open-design.ai**](https://open-design.ai/) or [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases)","- **Windows** (x64) → [**open-design.ai**](https://open-design.ai/) or [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases)","- **Linux** (AppImage, optional lane) → [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases)","","After install: the app auto-detects every coding-agent CLI on your `PATH`, loads 100+ skills and 150 design systems, and lets you type a brief in the entry view.","","### 🤖 Install into your coding agent (no UI)","","You can use Open Design without ever opening the GUI — call it as a skill, plugin, or MCP server inside Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, OpenClaw, Antigravity, Hermes, Kimi, and more.","","```bash","# One-line install into the agent you're using:","curl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh | sh -s \u003cagent\u003e","# \u003cagent\u003e = claude | codex | cursor | copilot | openclaw | antigravity | gemini","#         | pi | vibe | hermes | cline | kimi | trae | opencode","```","","Then, inside the agent:","","```","\u003e Use open-design to generate a landing page with the Linear design system","```","","The agent reads `skills/`, picks the right `SKILL.md`, binds the `DESIGN.md` you named, and emits an `\u003cartifact\u003e` previewable at `http://localhost:7456`.","","### 🐳 Run with Docker","","```bash","git clone https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design.git","cd open-design/deploy","cp .env.example .env","echo \"OD_API_TOKEN=$(openssl rand -hex 32)\" \u003e\u003e .env","docker compose up -d","# open http://localhost:7456","```","","\u003e **macOS users:** If the web UI shows `Authorization: Bearer \u003cOD_API_TOKEN\u003e required`, Docker Desktop bridge networking is the cause. See [Docker Desktop on macOS](deploy/README.md#docker-desktop-on-macos) for the fix.","","### 🧑‍💻 Run from source","","```bash","git clone https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design.git","cd open-design","corepack enable \u0026\u0026 pnpm install","pnpm tools-dev run web","```","","Node `~24`, pnpm `10.33.x`. Windows users, see [`docs/windows-troubleshooting.md`](docs/windows-troubleshooting.md). Full quickstart, env vars, Nix flake, and packaged build flow → [`QUICKSTART.md`](QUICKSTART.md).","","### A full workflow — from brief to artifact","","`brief → plugin → direction → design system → artifact → handoff → memory`","","1. **A PM submits a brief.** The plugin picker offers landing page · pitch deck · dashboard · social post · PM spec · OKR scorecard…","2. **A designer (or the agent) locks the direction.** No brand? Pick from 5 curated directions. Have a brand? Drop a screenshot / URL → the agent connects GitHub, imports Figma, and codifies a reusable `DESIGN.md`.","3. **The agent emits the first `\u003cartifact\u003e`.** Plugin + skill + `DESIGN.md` are bound. It streams into a sandboxed iframe, editable in place — not \"regenerate from scratch.\"","4. **Hand off to engineering.** The artifact is real HTML/CSS — drop it into Cursor, Codex, or Claude Code to keep building as code. Or export PPTX / PDF / MP4 straight to marketing.","5. **Open Design gets smarter as you use it.** Your screenshots, fonts, palettes, and confirmed artifacts accumulate as defaults for the next session. Less rework, less drift.","","---","","## Use Open Design from your coding agent","","Open Design ships a **stdio MCP server** and per-agent **install scripts**. Any MCP-compatible agent in another repo can read files from your local Open Design projects directly — tokens CSS, JSX components, entry HTML — as a structured API queryable by name. The agent always sees the live file, not a stale export.","","```bash","# One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):","curl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh | sh -s \u003cagent\u003e","","# Then the agent can:","od search-files \"primary button\"      # search files across projects","od get-file design-systems/linear-app/DESIGN.md","od get-artifact \u003cslug\u003e                # latest rendered artifact","od plugin run web-prototype --brief \"...\"","od skill list --scenario marketing","```","","**Why MCP?** Exporting and re-attaching a zip every iteration breaks flow. MCP exposes the design source directly — the agent always sees the live file.","","**For an agent starting from scratch,** the installer places `~/.config/\u003cagent\u003e/open-design.json` (or the platform equivalent) plus a copy-paste MCP snippet. Cursor gets a one-click deeplink; Claude Code gets a `claude mcp add-json` one-liner; every other agent gets JSON in the schema its config expects. Full per-agent flow → **Settings → MCP server** in the desktop app, or [`docs/agent-adapters.md`](docs/agent-adapters.md).","","**Security model.** Read-only by default, the daemon binds to `127.0.0.1`, and SSRF is blocked at the proxy edge. LAN exposure requires an explicit `OD_BIND_HOST` plus `OD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`. Connector credentials and live-artifact preview routes stay loopback-only regardless.","","---","","## Skills","","**100+ skills ship in the box** — each is a folder under [`skills/`](skills/) following the Claude Code [`SKILL.md`][skill] convention, extended with an `od:` frontmatter (`mode`, `platform`, `scenario`, `preview.type`, `design_system.requires`, `default_for`, `fidelity`, `example_prompt`). Drop a folder in, restart the daemon, it appears in the picker.","","Two **modes** anchor the catalog: `prototype` (web/mobile/desktop single-page artifacts) and `deck` (horizontal-swipe presentations). Also `image`, `video`, `audio`, `template`, `design-system`, and `utility` modes. The **`scenario`** field groups them by audience: `design` · `marketing` · `operation` · `engineering` · `product` · `finance` · `hr` · `sale` · `personal`.","","| Skill | Mode | Scenario | What it produces |","|---|---|---|---|","| [`web-prototype`](design-templates/web-prototype/) | prototype | design | Default landing page / hero |","| [`saas-landing`](design-templates/saas-landing/) | prototype | marketing | Hero / features / pricing / CTA |","| [`dashboard`](design-templates/dashboard/) | prototype | operation | Admin / analytics (with sidebar) |","| [`mobile-app`](design-templates/mobile-app/) | prototype | design | iPhone 15 Pro / Pixel framed app |","| [`mobile-onboarding`](design-templates/mobile-onboarding/) | prototype | design | Splash · value-prop · sign-in flow |","| [`social-carousel`](design-templates/social-carousel/) | prototype | marketing | 3-card 1080×1080 carousel |","| [`email-marketing`](design-templates/email-marketing/) | prototype | marketing | Table-fallback-safe brand email |","| [`magazine-poster`](design-templates/magazine-poster/) | prototype | marketing | Single-page magazine layout |","| [`motion-frames`](design-templates/motion-frames/) | prototype | marketing | Looping CSS motion hero |","| [`sprite-animation`](design-templates/sprite-animation/) | prototype | marketing | 8-bit Skill registry endpoint: `GET /api/skills`.","","---","","## Design Systems","","**150 brand-grade `DESIGN.md` systems** ship with the repo — each a single Markdown file with a 9-section schema (color, typography, spacing, layout, components, motion, voice, brand, anti-patterns), from [`VoltAgent/awesome-design-md`][acd2]. Switch a system → the next render uses the new tokens. No theme JSON.","","\u003cdetails\u003e","\u003csummary\u003e\u003cb\u003eFull catalog (click to expand)\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e","","**AI \u0026 LLM** — `claude` · `cohere` · `mistral-ai` · `minimax` · `together-ai` · `replicate` · `runwayml` · `elevenlabs` · `ollama` · `x-ai`","","**Developer Tools** — `cursor` · `vercel` · `linear-app` · `framer` · `expo` · `clickhouse` · `mongodb` · `supabase` · `hashicorp` · `posthog` · `sentry` · `warp` · `webflow` · `sanity` · `mintlify` · `lovable` · `composio` · `opencode-ai` · `voltagent`","","**Productivity** — `notion` · `figma` · `miro` · `airtable` · `superhuman` · `intercom` · `zapier` · `cal` · `clay` · `raycast`","","**Fintech** — `stripe` · `coinbase` · `binance` · `kraken` · `mastercard` · `revolut` · `wise`","","**E-commerce** — `shopify` · `airbnb` · `uber` · `nike` · `starbucks` · `pinterest`","","**Media** — `spotify` · `playstation` · `wired` · `theverge` · `meta`","","**Automotive** — `tesla` · `bmw` · `ferrari` · `lamborghini` · `bugatti` · `renault`","","**Other** — `apple` · `ibm` · `nvidia` · `vodafone` · `resend` · `spacex`","","**Starters** — `default` (Neutral Modern) · `warm-editorial`","","\u003c/details\u003e","","Re-import the library via [`scripts/sync-design-systems.ts`](scripts/sync-design-systems.ts). Add your own brand → drop a `DESIGN.md` into `design-systems/\u003cbrand\u003e/`. Full guide → [`design-systems/README.md`](design-systems/README.md).","","[acd2]: https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-design-md","","---","","## Plugins","","**261 official plugins** live in [`plugins/_official/`](plugins/_official/). Each plugin is a **portable agent-skill folder** — a `SKILL.md` (readable by any agent that supports Agent Skills), plus an optional `open-design.json` manifest that gives Open Design marketplace metadata, inputs, previews, pipelines, and capability declarations. Jump straight to a category:","","| Category | Count | Contents |","|---|---|---|","| [`scenarios/`](plugins/_official/scenarios/) | 11 | Complete design scenarios — [`od-default`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-default/), [`od-design-refine`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-design-refine/), [`od-figma-migration`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-figma-migration/), [`od-code-migration`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-code-migration/), [`od-react-export`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-react-export/), [`od-nextjs-export`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-nextjs-export/), [`od-vue-export`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-vue-export/), [`od-media-generation`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-media-generation/), [`od-new-generation`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-new-generation/), [`od-tune-collab`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-tune-collab/), [`od-plugin-authoring`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-plugin-authoring/) |","| [`image-templates/`](plugins/_official/image-templates/) | 45 | One-shot image prompts — editorial, cinematic, product, portrait |","| [`video-templates/`](plugins/_official/video-templates/) | 50 | HyperFrames / Seedance / Veo motion templates |","| [`design-systems/`](plugins/_official/design-systems/) | 142 | Brand `DESIGN.md` wrapped as plugins |","| [`atoms/`](plugins/_official/atoms/) | 13 | Reusable UI fragments (buttons, heroes, KPI cards) |","| [`examples/`](plugins/_official/examples/) | 140 | Remixable reference outputs |","","Also [`plugins/community/`](plugins/community/) for community plugins and [`plugins/registry/`](plugins/registry/) for the publishing flow.","","### What plugins can do","","- 🤖 **Run in any coding agent** — [Claude Code](docs/agent-adapters.md), Codex, Cursor, Copilot, [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw), [Antigravity](https://antigravity.google), Hermes, Kimi… through the same skill protocol the agent already knows.","- 🔁 **Migrate Figma / Pencil workflows** → React, Next.js, or Vue source. See [`od-figma-migration`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-figma-migration/).","- 🛠️ **Refresh an existing codebase to a brand spec** — point a plugin at a `git` repo + `DESIGN.md`, get a PR. See [`od-code-migration`](plugins/_official/scenarios/od-code-migration/).","- 💾 **Persist custom workflows** — your team's reusable templates sit next to the shipped ones.","","### Using plugins","","Plugins are at full parity across the **web UI** and the **`od` CLI** — same `/api/plugins` endpoints, pick whichever fits.","","**In the desktop / web app:** open the **Plugin** page to browse the marketplace and click **Install**; inside a project's Studio, plugins appear as composer chips you click to apply (with the inputs they declare).","","**On the command line** (runs without a UI — this is the path external agents use):","","```bash","od plugin list                       # list installed plugins (--task-kind / --mode / --tag filters)","od plugin search \"landing page\"      # search by keyword","od plugin info od-default            # inspect a plugin's metadata, inputs, capabilities","od plugin install od-figma-migration # install from a registry; also accepts ./local-folder or an https://… link","od plugin apply od-default --input brief=\"a one-page pitch for our seed round\"","od plugin upgrade od-default         # upgrade","od plugin uninstall od-default       # uninstall","```","","Every command supports `--json`, so you can pipe it through `jq` / `xargs` into automation.","","### Building a plugin","","A plugin **needs only a `SKILL.md` at minimum**; to list it in the Open Design marketplace, add an `open-design.json`:","","```","my-plugin/","├── SKILL.md            ← required: YAML frontmatter (name · description) + trigger phrasing + workflow (aim for \u003c 500 lines)","├── open-design.json    ← needed to list: marketplace metadata + inputs + pipeline + capabilities","├── README.md           ← optional: usage, install, registry links","├── preview/            ← optional: index.html / poster.png (strongly recommended for visual plugins)","└── examples/           ← optional: concrete use cases","```","","Core `open-design.json` fields: `specVersion` (currently `1.0.0`), `name` (stable ID), `version` (semver), `compat.agentSkills[].path` (points at `./SKILL.md`), `od.kind` (`skill` / `scenario` / `atom` / `bundle`), `od.taskKind` (`new-generation` / `figma-migration` / `code-migration` / `tune-collab`), `od.mode` (the output surface, e.g. `prototype` / `deck` / `live-artifact` / `image` / `video` / `hyperframes` / `audio` / `design-system` / `scenario`), `od.capabilities[]` (**declare the minimum** — a restricted install grants only `prompt:inject` by default), `od.inputs[]` (apply-time parameters).","","Scaffold + validate locally:","","```bash","od plugin scaffold --id my-plugin --title \"My Plugin\"   # generate the skeleton","od plugin validate ./my-plugin                          # check manifest / file layout","pnpm guard \u0026\u0026 pnpm --filter @open-design/plugin-runtime typecheck","```","","Full field set and runtime contract → [`plugins/spec/SPEC.md`](plugins/spec/SPEC.md); developing a plugin with a coding agent → [`plugins/spec/AGENT-DEVELOPMENT.md`](plugins/spec/AGENT-DEVELOPMENT.md); copy-paste minimal templates → [`plugins/spec/examples/`](plugins/spec/examples/).","","### Contributing a plugin","","1. Drop the plugin folder into [`plugins/community/`](plugins/community/) (third-party plugins), or — to ship it bundled with Open Design — into the matching tier of [`plugins/_official/`](plugins/_official/).","2. Pass validation: `od plugin validate`, `pnpm guard`, `pnpm --filter @open-design/plugin-runtime typecheck`.","3. Fill the PR using the template in [`plugins/spec/CONTRIBUTING.md`](plugins/spec/CONTRIBUTING.md) (ID, version, lane, mode, capabilities, trigger examples; attach a screenshot / preview for visual plugins).","4. To publish to an external registry (skills.sh / ClawHub / standalone GitHub) → [`plugins/spec/PUBLISHING-REGISTRIES.md`](plugins/spec/PUBLISHING-REGISTRIES.md).","","Plugin registry endpoint: `GET /api/plugins`. Directory overview → [`plugins/README.md`](plugins/README.md) ([简体中文](plugins/README.zh-CN.md)).","","---","","## Architecture","","```","┌────────────────── browser (Next.js 16) / Electron shell ──────────────┐","│  chat · file workspace · iframe preview · settings · import · MCP     │","└──────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────┘","               │ /api/*                              │","               ▼                                     ▼","   ┌─────────────────────────────────┐   /api/proxy/{provider}/stream (SSE)","   │  local daemon (Express+SQLite)  │   ─→ any OpenAI-compatible BYOK,","   │                                  │       SSRF-guarded at the edge","   │  /api/skills    /api/plugins    │","   │  /api/design-systems            │","   │  /api/chat (SSE)   /api/proxy/* │","   │  /api/projects/:id/files/...    │","   │  /api/artifacts/{save,lint}     │","   │  /api/import/claude-design      │","   │  MCP stdio server                │","   └─────────┬───────────────────────┘","             │ spawn(cli, [...], { cwd: .od/projects/\u003cid\u003e })","             ▼","   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐","   │  claude · codex · cursor-agent · copilot · openclaw · antigravity ·│","   │  gemini · opencode · qwen · qoder · hermes (ACP) · kimi (ACP) ·    │","   │  pi (RPC) · kiro · kilo · vibe (ACP) · cline · trae · deepseek     │","   │  reads SKILL.md + DESIGN.md, writes artifacts to disk             │","   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘","```","","| Layer | Stack |","|---|---|","| Frontend | Next.js 16 App Router + React 18 + TypeScript |","| Daemon | Node 24 · Express · SSE streaming · `better-sqlite3` |","| Storage | Files at `.od/projects/\u003cid\u003e/` + SQLite at `.od/app.sqlite` + `media-config.json` (gitignored, auto-created). `OD_DATA_DIR` relocates everything. |","| Preview | Sandboxed `srcdoc` iframe + streaming `\u003cartifact\u003e` parser |","| Export | HTML (inlined) · PDF (browser print) · PPTX (agent-driven) · ZIP · Markdown · MP4 (HyperFrames) |","| Desktop | Electron shell + sandboxed renderer + sidecar IPC (STATUS · EVAL · SCREENSHOT · CONSOLE · CLICK · SHUTDOWN) |","| Lifecycle | One entry point: `pnpm tools-dev` (start / stop / run / status / logs / inspect / check) |","","Full architecture → [`docs/architecture.md`](docs/architecture.md). Skill protocol → [`docs/skills-protocol.md`](docs/skills-protocol.md). Agent adapter contract → [`docs/agent-adapters.md`](docs/agent-adapters.md).","","---","","## Roadmap","","- [x] Daemon + 22 coding-agent CLI adapters + skill registry + design-system catalog","- [x] Web app + chat + question form + 5-direction picker + todo progress + sandboxed preview","- [x] 100+ skills · 150 design systems · 5 visual directions · 5 device frames","- [x] SQLite-backed projects · conversations · messages · tabs · templates","- [x] Multi-provider BYOK proxy (`/api/proxy/{anthropic,openai,azure,google,ollama,senseaudio}/stream`) + SSRF guard","- [x] Claude Design ZIP import (`/api/import/claude-design`)","- [x] Sidecar protocol + Electron desktop + IPC automation","- [x] Artifact lint API + 5-dim self-critique pre-emit gate","- [x] **0.8.0** — plugin marketplace infrastructure (261 official plugins, manifest spec, per-agent install scripts)","- [x] **0.9.0** — Open Design AMR (official Model Router built into the app: zero config, one-click sign-in)","- [x] Packaged Electron builds — macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) + Windows (x64) + Linux AppImage (optional lane)","- [ ] Comment-mode surgical edits — partially shipped; reliable targeted patching in progress","- [ ] AI-emitted tweaks panel UX — not yet implemented","- [ ] `npx od init` to scaffold a project with `DESIGN.md`","- [ ] Plugin SDK + `od plugin {add,list,remove,test,publish}` CLI","- [ ] Figma / Pencil → React / Next / Vue migration plugins (alpha)","- [ ] Refresh-existing-codebase plugin (point at a git repo + `DESIGN.md`)","","Phased delivery → [`docs/roadmap.md`](docs/roadmap.md).","","---","","## Community","","Real people behind every channel.","","- 💬 **Discord** — daily chat, plugin sharing, questions → [**discord.gg/qhbcCH8Am4**](https://discord.gg/qhbcCH8Am4)","- 🐦 **X / Twitter** — release notes, milestones, behind the scenes → [**@nexudotio**](https://x.com/nexudotio)","- 🗣️ **GitHub Discussions** — deep Q\u0026A, RFCs, \"show your work\" → [**Discussions**](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/discussions)","- 🐛 **GitHub Issues** — bug reports, feature requests → [**Issues**](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues)","","The [`good-first-issue`](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) and [`help-wanted`](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22help+wanted%22) labels are the easiest way in.","","---","","## Contributing","","Open Design keeps moving because contributors — designers, engineers, prompt authors — keep showing up. Many of the most-used skills, design systems, and plugins were written by people outside the core team.","","### 🎯 Where to start (max leverage, min change)","","| Want to ship… | How | Where |","|---|---|---|","| A new **skill** | Drop a folder with `SKILL.md` + `assets/` + `references/` | [`skills/`](skills/) · spec in [`docs/skills-protocol.md`](docs/skills-protocol.md) |","| A new **design system** | Drop a `DESIGN.md` using the 9-section schema | [`design-systems/\u003cbrand\u003e/`](design-systems/) |","| A new **plugin** | Drop `open-design.json` + manifest under a category folder | [`plugins/community/`](plugins/community/) · spec in [`plugins/spec/SPEC.md`](plugins/spec/SPEC.md) · agent dev guide in [`plugins/spec/AGENT-DEVELOPMENT.md`](plugins/spec/AGENT-DEVELOPMENT.md) |","| Support a new **coding-agent CLI** | One adapter entry + stream parser | [`apps/daemon/src/agents.ts`](apps/daemon/src/agents.ts) |","| Fix a bug or polish UI | Browse the [`good-first-issue`](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) label | [Issues →](https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues) |","| Translate the docs | Update the `README.\u003clang\u003e.md` files | [`TRANSLATIONS.md`](TRANSLATIONS.md) |","","### 🤖 Contributing as an agent","","If *you are the agent reading this*, the fastest path is:","","```bash","# 1. Boot locally","git clone https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design.git","cd open-design \u0026\u0026 corepack enable \u0026\u0026 pnpm install","pnpm tools-dev run web","","# 2. Find a good-first-issue and assign yourself","gh issue list --label \"good first issue\" --state open --limit 20","gh issue develop \u003cnumber\u003e   # create a branch and worktree","","# 3. Make the change, run the checks","pnpm guard \u0026\u0026 pnpm typecheck","pnpm --filter @open-design/\u003cpackage\u003e test","","# 4. Open the PR","gh pr create --fill","```","","Full agent-friendly contribution flow, code style, and PR bar → [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) ([Deutsch](docs/i18n/CONTRIBUTING.de.md) · [Français](docs/i18n/CONTRIBUTING.fr.md) · [简体中文](docs/i18n/CONTRIBUTING.zh-CN.md) · [日本語](docs/i18n/CONTRIBUTING.ja-JP.md) · [Português](docs/i18n/CONTRIBUTING.pt-BR.md)).","","### 🏅 Open Design Fellow program","","We're recruiting **Open Design Fellows** around the world — Fellows shape the product alongside the core team, represent Open Design officially in their region, and grow the community locally, backed by funded support ($1,000 / MR), free LLM credits, and a direct review track. <blockquote>
<p dir="auto">🔥 <strong>Open Design 0.10.0 is here: the all-in-one Agentic design workspace.</strong> The whole craft now lives in one window — go from a vague idea to discovering references, gathering material, editing interactively, queuing comments, polishing motion, and handing off to an editor or a Code Agent — without leaving the app. With parallel sessions, it stops feeling like one assistant and starts feeling like a local design team working for you. <a href="https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases">Download 0.10.0</a> · <a href="https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/discussions/4153" data-hovercard-type="discussion" data-hovercard-url="/nexu-io/open-design/discussions/4153/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">Join the discussion</a></p>
<p dir="auto">⚡ <strong>Open Design AMR (Agentic Model Router) — the official model service.</strong> One recharge to use GPT, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek inside Open Design: 20+ flagship models, zero config, billed by real token usage. <a href="https://open-design.ai/amr/" rel="nofollow">Try AMR</a></p>
<p dir="auto">🏅 <strong>The Open Design Fellow program is now open.</strong> If you also believe design should be open — become an Open Design Fellow, shape the product alongside the core team, and help more people take part in defining the future of design. Details → <a href="/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/MAINTAINERS.md"><code>MAINTAINERS.md</code></a> and <a href="https://discord.gg/qhbcCH8Am4" rel="nofollow">Discord</a>.</p>
</blockquote>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What is Open Design</h2><a id="user-content-what-is-open-design" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What is Open Design" href="#what-is-open-design"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">🎨 <strong>The local-first, open-source <a href="https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649" rel="nofollow">Claude Design</a> alternative.</strong>  🖥️ <strong>Native desktop app for macOS and Windows.</strong>  ⚡ <strong>100+ skills</strong> · ✨ <strong>150 brand-grade <code>DESIGN.md</code> systems</strong> · 📦 <strong>261 ready-to-use plugins.</strong>  🖼️ Generates <strong>web · desktop · mobile prototypes</strong>, <strong>live dashboards / artifacts</strong>, <strong>decks</strong>, <strong>images</strong>, <strong>video</strong>, plus <strong>HyperFrames</strong> motion graphics. 🔒 Sandboxed iframe preview · HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 export.  🤖 <strong>Runs on Claude Code · OpenClaw · Codex · Cursor · OpenCode · Qwen · Copilot · Amp · Hermes · Kimi · Antigravity and 22 local CLIs</strong>, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via BYOK.</p>
<p dir="auto">Open Design is what you get when the <strong>agent-native</strong> loop Anthropic shipped with Claude Design — discover the brief, lock the direction, stream the artifact, critique, deliver — stops being closed and becomes a <strong>filesystem of skills, design systems, and plugins</strong> that the coding agents already on your laptop can read, write, and remix. Your CLI becomes the design engine, your laptop becomes the studio, and your team's <code>DESIGN.md</code> becomes the brand contract.</p>
<p dir="auto">It's also the <strong>Figma alternative for the agent era</strong> — instead of pushing pixels on a canvas, it delivers single-page artifacts in real CSS, real fonts, real components, exported straight to HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 — already shaped by your design system, already runnable inside the agent you use every day.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Product tour</h2><a id="user-content-product-tour" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Product tour" href="#product-tour"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">A quick look at what Open Design is and what it does. Start from <strong>Home</strong>, orchestrate repeat workflows with <strong>Automation</strong>, distill a brand contract in <strong>Design System</strong>, and extend with <strong>Plugins</strong> and <strong>integrations</strong>; inside any project's <strong>Studio</strong>, the same design system streams out prototypes, live artifacts, HyperFrames, decks, and images.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Platform Compatibility</h2><a id="user-content-platform-compatibility" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Platform Compatibility" href="#platform-compatibility"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Open Design ships as <strong>skills, a CLI, and an MCP server</strong> that mainstream coding agents consume natively. Once OD is installed, a single <code>od mcp install <agent></code> wires the MCP server into that agent's config, and you call the same tools from inside any agent.</p>
</blockquote>

<p dir="auto"><code>od mcp install <agent> --print</code> for a dry-run preview · <code>--uninstall</code> to remove · full list with <code>od mcp install --help</code>.</p>
<p align="center" dir="auto">
  <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/67e5284a287c972b89b8e205f8f3a1c7ea5b104bc9ff178f5549e4010de5366d/68747470733a2f2f7265706f2d6173736574732e6f70656e2d64657369676e2e61692f7265736f75726365732f696d616765732f636f64696e672d6167656e74732e706e67"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/67e5284a287c972b89b8e205f8f3a1c7ea5b104bc9ff178f5549e4010de5366d/68747470733a2f2f7265706f2d6173736574732e6f70656e2d64657369676e2e61692f7265736f75726365732f696d616765732f636f64696e672d6167656e74732e706e67" alt="The 21 coding-agent CLIs Open Design supports — Claude Code · Codex · OpenCode · Hermes · Antigravity · Gemini · Grok Build · Kimi · Cursor Agent · Qwen · Qoder · GitHub Copilot · Pi · Kiro · Kilo · Mistral Vibe · DeepSeek · Reasonix · Aider · Devin · Trae" width="100%" data-canonical-src="https://repo-assets.open-design.ai/resources/images/coding-agents.png" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>
</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>No CLI installed?</strong> The BYOK proxy at <code>POST /api/proxy/{anthropic,openai,azure,google,ollama,senseaudio}/stream</code> gives you the same loop (no process spawn) — paste <code>baseUrl</code> + <code>apiKey</code> + <code>model</code>, with support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Per-target SSRF protection blocks internal IPs / link-local / CGNAT at the daemon edge.</p>
<p dir="auto">The adapter contract and stream parsers live in <a href="/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/apps/daemon/src/agents.ts"><code>apps/daemon/src/agents.ts</code></a>. Adding a new CLI is one entry — see <a href="/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/agent-adapters.md"><code>docs/agent-adapters.md</code></a>.</p>
<hr>

<p dir="auto"><strong>93 ready-to-replicate prompts</strong> live in <a href="/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/prompt-templates"><code>prompt-templates/</code></a> — preview thumbnails, full prompt body, target model, aspect ratio, and source attribution. One click drops a brief into the composer.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">5 · Video & HyperFrames — agent-native motion graphics</h3><a id="user-content-5--video--hyperframes--agent-native-motion-graphics" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 5 · Video & HyperFrames — agent-native motion graphics" href="#5--video--hyperframes--agent-native-motion-graphics"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong><a href="https://github.com/heygen-com/hyperframes">HyperFrames</a></strong> is HeyGen's open-source, agent-native video framework, integrated as a first-class citizen in Open Design. The agent writes HTML + CSS + GSAP, and HyperFrames renders it to a deterministic MP4 via headless Chrome + FFmpeg. Pair it with <strong>Seedance 2.0</strong> for cinematic t2v / i2v, <strong>Veo 3 / Sora 2 / Kling 2</strong> for routed model variants, and <strong>Suno v5 / Lyria 2</strong> for the audio layer.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why Open Design</h2><a id="user-content-why-open-design" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why Open Design" href="#why-open-design"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>In April 2026, Anthropic released <a href="https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649" rel="nofollow">Claude Design</a> — the first time an LLM stopped writing prose and started delivering design artifacts directly.</strong> It went viral. But it stayed closed-source, paid-only, cloud-only, locked to Anthropic's model, Anthropic's skills, Anthropic's surface. No checkout, no self-host, no Vercel deploy, no swap-in-your-own-agent.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Open Design (OD) is the open-source alternative. Same loop, same artifact-first mental model, none of the lock-in:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>🤖 <strong>Agent-native, model-agnostic.</strong> We don't ship an agent. The <code>claude</code> / <code>codex</code> / <code>cursor-agent</code> / <code>copilot</code> / <code>hermes</code> / <code>kimi</code> already on your <code>PATH</code> are the design engine. Swap with one click.</li>
<li>🧠 <strong>Brand-grade by default.</strong> Every render reads the active <code>DESIGN.md</code> — a 9-section schema covering palette, type, spacing, motion, voice, anti-patterns. 150 systems ship with the repo (Linear, Stripe, Vercel, Airbnb, Apple, Tesla, Notion, Anthropic, Cursor, Supabase, Figma…). Drop a folder in, the picker finds it.</li>
<li>🖥️ <strong>Local-first, BYOK at every layer.</strong> Native desktop apps for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) and Windows (x64). Linux AppImage on the optional release lane. SQLite at <code>.od/app.sqlite</code>, files at <code>.od/projects/<id>/</code>, no telemetry, no cloud round-trip.</li>
<li>🌍 <strong>Composable on three planes.</strong> <strong>Plugins</strong> carry runnable workflows · <strong>skills</strong> carry the agent's design taste · <strong>design systems</strong> carry the brand. All three are plain files anyone can author, version, and publish.</li>
<li>🔁 <strong>Refresh an existing codebase.</strong> Hand a <code>git</code> repo + <code>DESIGN.md</code> to the agent and it refactors your real components to the brand spec. Dedicated plugins migrate Figma / Pencil workflows into React / Next.js / Vue code.</li>
<li>🔒 <strong>Privacy by conviction.</strong> Everything runs where your data lives — your laptop, your team's server, your Vercel project. When the network is needed, the BYOK proxy is SSRF-guarded.</li>
</ul>

<p dir="auto">The fastest way to use Open Design. No Node, no pnpm, no clone.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>macOS</strong> (Apple Silicon · Intel x64) → <a href="https://open-design.ai/" rel="nofollow"><strong>open-design.ai</strong></a> or <a href="https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases">GitHub Releases</a></li>
<li><strong>Windows</strong> (x64) → <a href="https://open-design.ai/" rel="nofollow"><strong>open-design.ai</strong></a> or <a href="https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases">GitHub Releases</a></li>
<li><strong>Linux</strong> (AppImage, optional lane) → <a href="https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases">GitHub Releases</a></li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">After install: the app auto-detects every coding-agent CLI on your <code>PATH</code>, loads 100+ skills and 150 design systems, and lets you type a brief in the entry view.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🤖 Install into your coding agent (no UI)</h3><a id="user-content--install-into-your-coding-agent-no-ui" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🤖 Install into your coding agent (no UI)" href="#-install-into-your-coding-agent-no-ui"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">You can use Open Design without ever opening the GUI — call it as a skill, plugin, or MCP server inside Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, OpenClaw, Antigravity, Hermes, Kimi, and more.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">A full workflow — from brief to artifact</h3><a id="user-content-a-full-workflow--from-brief-to-artifact" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: A full workflow — from brief to artifact" href="#a-full-workflow--from-brief-to-artifact"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><code>brief → plugin → direction → design system → artifact → handoff → memory</code></p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>A PM submits a brief.</strong> The plugin picker offers landing page · pitch deck · dashboard · social post · PM spec · OKR scorecard…</li>
<li><strong>A designer (or the agent) locks the direction.</strong> No brand? Pick from 5 curated directions. Have a brand? Drop a screenshot / URL → the agent connects GitHub, imports Figma, and codifies a reusable <code>DESIGN.md</code>.</li>
<li><strong>The agent emits the first <code><artifact></code>.</strong> Plugin + skill + <code>DESIGN.md</code> are bound. It streams into a sandboxed iframe, editable in place — not "regenerate from scratch."</li>
<li><strong>Hand off to engineering.</strong> The artifact is real HTML/CSS — drop it into Cursor, Codex, or Claude Code to keep building as code. Or export PPTX / PDF / MP4 straight to marketing.</li>
<li><strong>Open Design gets smarter as you use it.</strong> Your screenshots, fonts, palettes, and confirmed artifacts accumulate as defaults for the next session. Less rework, less drift.</li>
</ol>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Use Open Design from your coding agent</h2><a id="user-content-use-open-design-from-your-coding-agent" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Use Open Design from your coding agent" href="#use-open-design-from-your-coding-agent"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Open Design ships a <strong>stdio MCP server</strong> and per-agent <strong>install scripts</strong>. Any MCP-compatible agent in another repo can read files from your local Open Design projects directly — tokens CSS, JSX components, entry HTML — as a structured API queryable by name. The agent always sees the live file, not a stale export.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):</span>
curl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh <span class="pl-k">|</span> sh -s <span class="pl-k"><</span>agent<span class="pl-k">></span>

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Then the agent can:</span>
od search-files <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>primary button<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>      <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> search files across projects</span>
od get-file design-systems/linear-app/DESIGN.md
od get-artifact <span class="pl-k"><</span>slug<span class="pl-k">></span>                <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> latest rendered artifact</span>
od plugin run web-prototype --brief <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>...<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
od skill list --scenario marketing</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):
curl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh | sh -s <agent>

# Then the agent can:
od search-files "primary button"      # search files across projects
od get-file design-systems/linear-app/DESIGN.md
od get-artifact <slug>                # latest rendered artifact
od plugin run web-prototype --brief "..."
od skill list --scenario marketing" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><strong>Why MCP?</strong> Exporting and re-attaching a zip every iteration breaks flow. MCP exposes the design source directly — the agent always sees the live file.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>For an agent starting from scratch,</strong> the installer places <code>~/.config/<agent>/open-design.json</code> (or the platform equivalent) plus a copy-paste MCP snippet. Cursor gets a one-click deeplink; Claude Code gets a <code>claude mcp add-json</code> one-liner; every other agent gets JSON in the schema its config expects. Full per-agent flow → <strong>Settings → MCP server</strong> in the desktop app, or <a href="/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/agent-adapters.md"><code>docs/agent-adapters.md</code></a>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Security model.</strong> Read-only by default, the daemon binds to <code>127.0.0.1</code>, and SSRF is blocked at the proxy edge. LAN exposure requires an explicit <code>OD_BIND_HOST</code> plus <code>OD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS</code>. Connector credentials and live-artifact preview routes stay loopback-only regardless.</p>
<hr>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Skills</h2><a id="user-content-skills" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Skills" href="#skills"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>100+ skills ship in the box</strong> — each is a folder under <a href="/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/skills"><code>skills/</code></a> following the Claude Code <a href="https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/skills" rel="nofollow"><code>SKILL.md</code></a> convention, extended with an <code>od:</code> frontmatter (<code>mode</code>, <code>platform</code>, <code>scenario</code>, <code>preview.type</code>, <code>design_system.requires</code>, <code>default_for</code>, <code>fidelity</code>, <code>example_prompt</code>). Drop a folder in, restart the daemon, it appears in the picker.</p>
<p dir="auto">Two <strong>modes</strong> anchor the catalog: <code>prototype</code> (web/mobile/desktop single-page artifacts) and <code>deck</code> (horizontal-swipe presentations). Also <code>image</code>, <code>video</code>, <code>audio</code>, <code>template</code>, <code>design-system</code>, and <code>utility</code> modes. The <strong><code>scenario</code></strong> field groups them by audience: <code>design</code> · <code>marketing</code> · <code>operation</code> · <code>engineering</code> · <code>product</code> · <code>finance</code> · <code>hr</code> · <code>sale</code> · <code>personal</code>.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Design Systems</h2><a id="user-content-design-systems" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Design Systems" href="#design-systems"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>150 brand-grade <code>DESIGN.md</code> systems</strong> ship with the repo — each a single Markdown file with a 9-section schema (color, typography, spacing, layout, components, motion, voice, brand, anti-patterns), from <a href="https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-design-md"><code>VoltAgent/awesome-design-md</code></a>. Switch a system → the next render uses the new tokens. No theme JSON.</p>
